---
title: "ST512-SP26-Homework-4"
author: "Jason Menjivar-Hernandez"
format: pdf
---

## Problem 1

**a**

| Source    | DF  | SS      | MS     | F-value | p-value  |
|-----------|-----|---------|--------|---------|----------|
| Treatment | 4   | 1010.56 | 252.4  | 29.84   | \<0.0001 |
| Block     | 5   | 323.82  | 64.765 | 7.65    | 0.004    |
| Error     | 20  | 169.33  | 8.4665 |         |          |
| Total     | 29  | 1503.71 |        |         |          |

**b**

6 blocks were used in this experiment.

**c**

$Y_{ij} = \mu + \alpha_i + \beta_j + \epsilon_{ij}$

$Y_{ij}$= observation for treatment i in block j

$\mu$ = overall mean

$\alpha_i$ = effect due to the i-th level of the factor, $\sum \alpha_i = 0$

$\beta_j$= effect due to the j-th block effect, $\sum \beta_j = 0$

$\epsilon_{ij}$ = random error ~N(0, $\sigma^2)$

Assumptions: equal variance, independence, normality, additivity 

**d** 

Both treatment and block effects are significant. This means that the treatment means are not equal. Block means were also different. The blocking helped reduce experimental variability. 

## R code

**problem 1**

```{r}
1-pf(29.84,4,20) #part a

1-pf(7.65, 5, 20) #part a 
```
